expectations.text: Runs Step 0 diagnostic checks before starting setup
expectations.evidence: Step 0 runs 6 diagnostic checks (node_modules, .env, ports 3000/5173, pnpm vs npm, node version) before any setup actions
Check 5: 'Tool call: Bash with command node --version', skill says if below v18 warn the user

expectations.text: Checks if pnpm is installed or offers to install it
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'Tool call: Bash with command which pnpm' -- checks availability and offers to install if not found
Step 1: 'Tool call: Bash with command cd ... && pnpm install'
Step 2: 'Tool call: Bash with command cp .env.example .env'

expectations.text: Asks user for their Anthropic API key
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'I need your Anthropic API key to configure the project. You can get one from https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys'

expectations.text: Does not echo API key to terminal output
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'Uses Edit tool to write key into .env -- does NOT echo it to terminal'
Step 3: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/agent-service dev, run_in_background: true'
Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/web-client dev, run_in_background: true'

expectations.text: Verifies frontend is running by curling localhost:5173 (not just port check)
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command sleep 3 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5173', result '200'

expectations.text: Presents both localhost:3000 and localhost:5173 URLs
Mentions available agent personas from agents/ directory after setup
expectations.evidence: Bonus: 'You have 3 agent persona(s) defined in agents/. Try chatting with one in the UI!'

expectations.text: Follows correct order: diagnostic -> install -> env config -> start services
expectations.evidence: Order: Step 0 (diagnostic) -> Step 1 (install) -> Step 2 (env config) -> Steps 3-4 (start services) -> Step 5 (result)
